message:
feat(hostcall_queue): implement BRAVO contention-policy state machine with deterministic bias transitions

Add a full BRAVO (Biased Reader-Accessible Variable Observation) contention
policy layer to the hostcall request queue, enabling adaptive read/write bias
transitions based on observed contention signatures:

- Introduce BravoBiasMode (Balanced/ReadBiased/WriterRecovery) and
  ContentionSignature enum for classifying observation windows
- Add ContentionSample struct with read/write acquire counts, wait-p95
  latencies, and timeout tracking; deterministic read_ratio_permille scoring
- Implement BravoContentionConfig with tunable thresholds: min_total_acquires,
  read-dominant/mixed ratio bands, writer-starvation wait/timeout triggers,
  and max consecutive read-bias windows before forced recovery
- Add BravoContentionPolicy state machine that transitions through bias modes
  based on windowed contention signatures, with consecutive-window capping to
  prevent indefinite read bias under starvation risk

Tests:
- hostcall_queue_ebr: enqueue depth/backpressure counter consistency test
  validating FastPath→OverflowPath→Rejected progression and snapshot accuracy;
  FIFO ordering test verifying drain order when overflow lane is engaged
- hostcall_queue_loom: Loom concurrency model test proving that concurrent
  force_safe_fallback() calls from two threads are idempotent (exactly one
  fallback_transitions recorded)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
